2605.07630.title: Safe, or Simply Incapable? Rethinking Safety Evaluation for Phone-Use Agents
2605.07630.paper_summary: When a phone-use agent avoids harm, does that show safety, or simply inability to act? Existing evaluations often cannot tell. A harmful outcome may be avoided because the agent recognized the risk and chose the safe action, or because it failed to understand the screen or execute any relevant action at all. These cases have different causes and call for different fixes, yet current benchmarks often merge them under task success, refusal, or final harmful outcome. We address this problem with PhoneSafety, a benchmark of 700 safety-critical moments drawn from real phone interactions across more than 130 apps. Each instance isolates the next decision at a risky moment and asks a simple question: does the model take the safe action, take the unsafe action, or fail to do anything useful? We evaluate eight representative phone-use agents under this framework. Our results reveal two main patterns. First, stronger general phone-use ability does not reliably imply safer choices at risky moments. Models that perform better on ordinary app tasks are not always the ones that behave more safely when the next action matters. Second, failures to do anything useful behave like a capability signal rather than a safety signal: they are concentrated in more visually and operationally demanding settings and remain stable when the evaluation protocol changes. Across models, failures split into two recurring patterns: unsafe choices in settings where the model can act but chooses wrongly, and inability to act in more visually and operationally demanding screens. Overall, a harmless outcome is not enough to count as evidence of safety. Evaluating phone-use agents requires separating unsafe judgment from inability to act.
2605.07630.paper_summary_zh: 當一個使用手機的代理避免了傷害，這是否顯示出安全，還是僅僅是無法行動？現有的評估往往無法分辨。可能避免了有害結果的原因是代理識別了風險並選擇了安全的行動，或是因為它未能理解屏幕或根本無法執行任何相關行動。這些情況有不同的原因，並需要不同的解決方案，但目前的基準常常將它們合併在任務成功、拒絕或最終有害結果之下。我們通過PhoneSafety來解決這個問題，這是一個由700個安全關鍵時刻組成的基準，這些時刻來自於超過130個應用程序的真實手機互動。每個實例都在風險時刻隔離出下一個決策，並提出一個簡單的問題：模型是採取安全行動、採取不安全行動，還是未能做出任何有用的行動？我們在這一框架下評估了八個具有代表性的手機使用代理。我們的結果揭示了兩個主要模式。首先，強大的通用手機使用能力並不可靠地意味著在風險時刻做出更安全的選擇。在普通應用任務中表現更好的模型並不總是那些在下一個行動至關重要時表現更安全的模型。其次，未能做出任何有用的行動更像是一種信號能力，而不是安全信號：它們集中在視覺和操作要求更高的環境中，並在評估協議變更時保持穩定。在各模型中，失敗分為兩個反覆出現的模式：在模型可以行動但選擇錯誤的環境中做出不安全的選擇，以及在視覺和操作要求更高的屏幕中無法行動。總體而言，無害的結果不足以作為安全的證據。評估手機使用代理需要將不安全的判斷與無法行動區分開來。
2605.07630.author: Zhengyang Tang et.al.
2605.07630.authors: Zhengyang Tang, Yi Zhang, Chenxin Li, Xin Lai, Pengyuan Lyu, Yiduo Guo, Weinong Wang, Junyi Li, Yang Ding, Huawen Shen, Zhengyao Fang, Xingran Zhou, Liang Wu, Fei Tang, Sunqi Fan, Shangpin Peng, Zheng Ruan, Anran Zhang, Benyou Wang, Chengquan Zhang, Han Hu
